releases.summary: Add a family-aware ContractSpec Adoption Engine, expand contract authoring targets across CLI and VS Code tooling, and refresh release-facing schema and policy artifacts for downstream workspaces.

audiences.summary: Maintainers get a shared adoption catalog and resolver, Connect adoption hooks, MCP exposure for reuse decisions, expanded authoring-target coverage, and updated static policy artifacts.
Integrators can resolve existing workspace or ContractSpec OSS surfaces before adding new implementations, and can scaffold more contract families from the CLI and VS Code extension.

Customer workspaces gain setup-managed adoption guidance, Connect `adoption sync/resolve` flows, stronger runtime-import and deprecated-monolith guardrails, and updated bundled schemas in the published CLI entrypoint.

migrationInstructions.summary: ContractSpec workspaces can now opt into family-aware reuse guidance and local catalog sync through `connect.adoption`.

steps: Add or review `.contractsrc.json > connect.adoption`.
Run `contractspec connect adoption sync --json` to mirror the local catalog.
Route uncertain reuse decisions through `contractspec connect adoption resolve --family <family> --stdin`.

migrationInstructions.title: Use the expanded authoring-target surface in CLI and VS Code flows
migrationInstructions.summary: Shared workspace discovery and IDE/CLI create flows now recognize additional contract families beyond the original core set.
migrationInstructions.when: When using `contractspec create`, VS Code create commands, or custom authoring-target integrations.
steps: Use the new create targets for capability, policy, translation, visualization, job, agent, product-intent, harness scenario, and harness suite scaffolds where appropriate.
Update any custom file-name or directory assumptions to rely on shared authoring-target helpers instead of hard-coded extensions.

upgradeSteps.summary: The consumer plugin and Connect CLI now expose adoption-aware hook events in addition to contracts-spec review hooks.
instructions: Install or reference the `contractspec-adoption` marketplace/plugin assets where Connect hooks are consumed.
Use `contractspec connect hook adoption before-file-edit|before-shell-execution|after-file-edit --stdin` in host hook wiring.

upgradeSteps.summary: Generated Biome policy artifacts now flag deprecated monolith usage and obvious deep runtime entrypoint imports.

audiences.summary: Repeated local builds now hit Turbo cache after an initial run because generated timestamps, temporary TypeScript configs, generated roots, and source handlers no longer poison task hashes.
Web LLM guide generation is now a modeled Turbo task and generated docs metadata is stable, making downstream build behavior easier to reason about in local and CI workspaces.

Fresh `contractspec init --preset builder-local` workspaces now include the Builder API base URL in both `.contractsrc.json` and VS Code settings, while older local-only configs still work through the hosted API fallback.

Integrators can adopt canonical bootstrap presets, register trusted local daemons, and consume richer snapshot-backed mobile review and operator posture data.

releases.summary: Unify release authoring around guided capsules, canonical generated artifacts, and manifest-backed changelog surfaces.

audiences.summary: Release preparation now runs through guided `contractspec release prepare` and `contractspec release edit` flows, generated release artifacts become the canonical source of truth, and stable publish automation creates a GitHub Release from patch notes plus attached release artifacts.
The public changelog now renders canonical release summaries, deprecations, migration guidance, and upgrade steps directly from the generated release manifest instead of scraping package changelogs.
Existing tooling that depended on `contractspec changelog` or on the `@contractspec/action.version` changelog side effect must move to `contractspec release prepare` plus `generated/releases/*`.

releases.summary: Implement ContractSpec Connect as a first-class spec, runtime, and CLI workflow.

audiences.summary: Connect is now a governed repo surface with CLI commands, workspace services, and versioned docs that keep risky edits, review packets, and replay artifacts aligned.

Update `scripts/generate-example-registry.ts` if the package needs a non-default preview component export.
Regenerate the example registries before publishing.

evidence: `@contractspec/module.examples` now exports a generated inline preview registry plus helpers for public example docs and sandbox fallback URLs.
`@contractspec/bundle.marketing` no longer maintains its own inline preview allowlist; it consumes the shared registry for `/templates`.
`@contractspec/app.web-landing` now derives `/docs/examples/*` routes and sitemap entries from the public example registry instead of a curated hardcoded subset.
`@contractspec/example.agent-console` now exposes a shared `AgentConsolePreview` component that web and mobile render through the UI kit alias layer.

`@contractspec/app.expo-demo` exposes the same discoverable example catalog through first-class mobile Examples navigation, keeps `/examples-preview` as a compatibility alias, renders every example in-app, and uses the shared Agent Console preview instead of a separate mobile-only summary panel.

releases.summary: Add versioning-backed release capsules, generated patch notes, and guided upgrade flows.

audiences.summary: Release communication is now generated from versioning-backed release capsules and enforced on release branches.
Guided upgrade plans and agent prompts now come from generated upgrade manifests instead of ad hoc prose.
Web changelog consumers can prefer generated release manifests while older package changelogs remain supported as fallback.

deprecations: The standalone release domain under `@contractspec/lib.contracts-spec/release` is deprecated in favor of versioning-owned release metadata.

migrationInstructions.title: Add .release.yaml companions for changesets
migrationInstructions.summary: Published release changesets now require a structured release capsule.
migrationInstructions.when: When preparing release branches, publish workflows, or release communication bundles.
steps: Create `.changeset/<slug>.release.yaml` next to each published changeset.
Record customer impact, migration notes, validation commands, and evidence in the release capsule.
Run `contractspec release build` before `contractspec release check --strict`.

upgradeSteps.title: Scaffold release capsule companions
upgradeSteps.summary: Add release capsules for changesets and include validation evidence.
instructions: Run `contractspec release init` for new release work.
Keep `.changeset/*.md` and `.changeset/*.release.yaml` together in the same PR.
Use `contractspec release brief` or `contractspec upgrade prompt` to generate maintainer, customer, and agent guidance.

upgradeSteps.title: Use generated release manifests in tooling
upgradeSteps.summary: Prefer generated release artifacts for changelog and upgrade flows.
instructions: Run `contractspec release build` to populate `generated/releases/`.
Point changelog or upgrade tooling at `generated/releases/manifest.json` and `generated/releases/upgrade-manifest.json`.

cd packages/bundles/workspace && bun test src/services/versioning/release-service.test.ts src/services/upgrade/guided-upgrade.test.ts && bun run typecheck && bun run build
cd packages/apps/cli-contractspec && bun run typecheck && bun run build
cd packages/apps/web-landing && bun run build:types
evidence: Release artifacts and upgrade prompts are generated from versioning-backed schemas and bundle.workspace services.
Guided upgrade manifest resolution now honors the current package root inside parent monorepos.

releases.summary: Keep the VS Code extension production typecheck focused on runtime sources while allowing Bun-typed workspace imports to resolve.

audiences.summary: The extension build now excludes Bun test files from the production typecheck and includes Bun ambient types for workspace source imports that expose Bun adapters.
VS Code extension packaging no longer fails when workspace source imports reference Bun globals through shared ContractSpec packages.
This patch restores extension build reliability without changing runtime extension behavior.

upgradeSteps.title: Rebuild the VS Code extension package
upgradeSteps.summary: Re-run the extension build so the corrected TypeScript boundary is applied before packaging.
instructions: Run `bun run --cwd packages/apps/vscode-contractspec build`.
Package or publish the extension from the rebuilt output.

bun run --cwd packages/apps/vscode-contractspec build
cd packages/apps/vscode-contractspec && node ../../../scripts/biome.cjs check tsconfig.json
evidence: The extension typecheck excludes `src/**/*.test.ts`, so Bun test imports are not part of the production build program.
The extension typecheck includes Bun ambient types, so transitive workspace source files that reference Bun globals resolve cleanly.
The VS Code extension bundle completed successfully after the TypeScript boundary fix.
